title: Backprop in Plain English
description: A junior engineer just finished a Coursera ML course and asks why backprop matters. Keep it intuitive.

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: I just finished the gradient descent module. What's the deal with backprop, exactly?

templates.text: Think of it as the trick that makes training huge networks even possible.
templates.hint_zh: 把它想成讓大型網路訓練變得可行的關鍵技巧。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Without it we couldn't train deep models?

templates.text: Not at scale. Naive chain rule would blow up exponentially. Backprop walks layer by layer and reuses results.
templates.hint_zh: 規模一上去就不行。樸素鏈式法則會指數爆炸,反向傳播是逐層走、複用中間結果。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: So it's just the chain rule, applied smartly?

templates.text: Exactly. It's a special case of reverse-mode automatic differentiation.
templates.hint_zh: 正是。它是反向模式自動微分的一個特例。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: What about the vanishing gradient thing I keep hearing about?

templates.text: Real problem. When gradients shrink layer by layer, early layers stop learning. ReLU and skip connections were the main fixes.
templates.hint_zh: 是真問題。梯度逐層縮小,前面幾層就學不動。ReLU 跟殘差連接是主要解法。

turns.speaker: bot
turns.text: Got it. Anything else worth knowing as a beginner?

templates.text: You almost never write backprop yourself. PyTorch and JAX do it for you. But knowing the shape helps debug weird training behavior.
templates.hint_zh: 你幾乎不會自己寫反向傳播,PyTorch 跟 JAX 都會代勞。但懂概念輪廓對排查訓練怪象很有用。
